text: Ha ha your right there Rob. I once met or should I say was near Bernard Cribbins and for some reason before that I didn't warm to him., how wrong was I, he was filming a children's series about a retired sailor and between takes he had all the kids around him and spent so much time with them. I have sung in many choirs over the years and we used to get asked to sing in pubs especially Christmas time no mention of a fee of course and guess what always on a night when there are few customers. 15 Welsh men drinking 4 or more pints each mmmm. that will keep the lights on.

author: @Oli-l5m
text:
I wasn't sure I would like this video, but it was certainly worth watching. 
When I was younger, especially as someone who likes to do whatever I can to help people out, I think I learned the hard way how to spot users, but rather than become bitter about it, I took the experience and added it to the pile of knowledge we call life! 

Unfortunately, I get the feeling the people who need this advice are not going to be thinking they need it!

author: @pedjamilosavljevic6235
text: In my opinion , such things should be taught from elementary school - onward (and within family , by parents , or caretakers ). I disagree only in one point - most despicable people are traitors (in general , in lawful circumstances and not only in national sense). Open source work is - open source work - it is a collaboration with a common goal. Help , in a form of guidance , is help to a particular individual , which has a particular problem to solve and limited , or any knowledge in a particular field (providing that it is not done to a personal detriment , like loosing enormous  amount of time doing it) - within a reason. Business is thing where people tend to learn very fast - first time they get screwed over , without possibility to reach justice (they didn't read small print , for example , or they trusted some person , that he , or she wouldn't do something immoral and take advantage of circumstances). As for "users" , or "abusers" , I wouldn't even comment on them - accept , they play on good side of human nature - really despicable .

author: @johnransom1146
text: Who would ever think that you’re a simpleton?

author: @0101-f4d
text: "Chimpanzees at a tea party throwing faeces at each other"🤣I do wish you'd give us autistics a break though, we just have a different brain chemistry to you and I feel that you are aggressively dismissive of something we have no control over. Would you lecture someone with no legs for not keeping up with you in a race? You are forever extolling the virtues of living and letting live yet you speak with such venom about people who care about the minutia, I think you often ascribe malice where divergent neurochemistry would be a better explanation.

author: @Cumbriman
text: I wouldn't have thought many people would be successful in taking advantage of this fella he seems much too intelligent and wise. I really appreciate the fact that he shares a lot of his thoughts and philosophy as well as the videos as I think that in itself is quite a brave thing to do, to be so open and therefore vulnerable to criticism on a public platform. I think that this added openness, advice and sharing of experience is what makes this channel different and stand out from others and gives it that extra something that makes it warmer and a pleasure to watch and listen to.

author: @scotttovey
text:
In addition to Non Discloser Agreement's,  None Compete Agreements are just as dangerous.

Back in 2006, during the recession; the New York Times had a story on their site in which a college student had signed a non compete order while working as an intern for a company to complete his degree.

The individual made the mistake of mentioning to his intern employer that a company in a completely different industry and state, had offered him $75,000. After he disclosed that information, the company exercised the non compete agreement and prevented him from obtaining the job. 

The reason the evil employer exercised the non compete agreement is because the individuals supervisor, believed that a college grad should not be able to start his career at $75,000.

Since then, I consider all such agreements forms of slavery.
